_description: Rules applied per question, France vs Spain SF 2026-07-14. Tier labels per KNOCKOUT_STAGE_PRICING_DEEP_DIVE.md; cluster references per WINNING_PATTERNS_SYNTHESIS.md; design #1 = joint simulation per ML_MODEL_BUILD_RESEARCH_2026-07-13.md C.3/C.4.

per_question.note: Empirical lambda 3.17 (FRA 1.17 + ESP 2.00, Spain remarkably stable 1-3 every game) -> Poisson P(4+)=0.39. Offside family is a documented weak spot (net -75.99 RBP deviating from crowd). Crowd unobservable but historically sits 53-59 on these; anchored at 0.45, within 10pp of that band, tilted toward the unusually solid two-sided empirical evidence (not a one-team-outlier read).

Smarkets Cards O/U 3.5 normalized 0.4044 (tight book 0.388/0.424). Both teams empirically very clean (FRA 0.67, ESP 0.83 yc/game) but the market prices referee assignment and SF stakes we cannot see; the sim card layer reproduces all three card lines within 1pp. Stay at market.

Market mid 0.3205 (bid 0.3077, real two-sided book). Personal drought 1/6 S/A and worsening; re-derived from current log per the FRA-MAR Diaz precedent, not replayed from POR-ESP. Cluster-A (7-for-7, +144.67 RBP) says go below market on a genuine multi-game personal drought: 0.25. Not lower: he leads Spain's first-scorer market and shot volume is intact -- this is conversion drought, not absence from play.

No settled precedent anywhere in the record; submitting a modest low-confidence base rate instead of skipping (the FRA-MAR Q15 skip cost an estimated 220+ RBP win). Base: ~1 near-certain OFR in 12 team-games observed + WC-era OFR rate ~0.3/match + ET exposure (30% of sims) + SF scrutiny -> 0.33; nudged to 0.35 acknowledging the crowd likely sits higher and this is our lowest-confidence number.
Three independent anchors agree: FTR Draw 0.3012 (liquid), ET market 0.2967, sim 0.3010.

First break measured at 23-25' across all 12 games (median 24). Goal1-time-bracket market implies P(first goal <=24') = 0.4331; sim (timing slope fitted to that same market) gives 0.4327. Early-window timing is the validated side of the timing family (100%, +17.57).

per_question.text: first goal by single-digit shirt (1-9), incl ET, No if 0-0 after ET
per_question.note: Two independent estimates agree: market-normalized first-scorer share of single-digit players 0.2853 (16 quoted names mapped to QF squad numbers; Mbappe is #10 and NOT single-digit) and empirical tournament scorer share 0.31. x P(any goal incl ET)=0.970 x own-goal haircut -> 0.283. Biggest single-digit threats: Thuram #9, Dembele #7, Ferran #7, Oyarzabal is #21 (not eligible).

Smarkets To-qualify normalized: Spain 0.4127 (liquid two-sided). Sim calibrated to it (pens leg implied P(Spain wins shootout)=0.31, plausible given France won their only shootout-pressure moments and market prices France ET strength). Logical check: 0.41 >= P(Spain win reg)=0.297. RULE14 pass.

Smarkets anytime scorer 0.4405, tightest player book on the slate (0.4386/0.4425). Form red-hot (5/6) but the market sees it too; the one regime caution (2022 SF: Morocco held him to 0 SOT) was already spent last match and he scored anyway. Submit at market+: 0.45. Do NOT extremize past belief (STRATEGIC_MARGIN_PUSH).

No direct market. Sim: 0.7234 (window is 43 minutes of rising intensity; lambda_window ~1.28). Historically TIMING_NO_MARKET is the worst category (-63.72) BUT that was for self-built LATE-window decompositions; this window is mid-match, the sim is calibrated to the first-goal bracket market, and the marginal is high because ANY of ~2.7 expected goals landing in a 43-min window suffices. Shaded 0.72->0.71 for the category's history.

No Spain team-SOT market. Derived market-implied lambda: total SOT line (9.67) minus France line (5.02) = 4.65 -> P(5+)=0.4929. Empirical says 5/6 games >=5 SOT, but upward blends from thin markets are 0-for-5 / -155.64 RBP (retired RULE17); the one sub-5 game was vs Uruguay, the only elite-defense opponent Spain has faced -- France is that regime. Stay at derived market, tiny downward regime tilt: 0.48.
Smarkets BTTS normalized 0.5905 (liquid); sim 0.575. Market. (The BRA-NOR decomposition method is unnecessary when a liquid direct market exists.)

Race model on own-first-sub minutes: FRA [80,68,65,75,61,71] (never before 61' all tournament -- a stable manager habit), ESP [71,45,60,71,75,55] -> P(Spain first)=0.667 raw. RULE12 outlier check: dropping Spain's 45' blowout-rotation game gives 0.60 (<10pp move, passes). Shrunk to 0.60 for n=6 and game-state confounding (both teams usually led; an even SF may delay both).

Market quotes the 4.5 line (P(5+)=0.5633) -> fitted lambda 5.02 -> P(4+)=0.7358 Poisson; NB overdispersion shades it to ~0.72. This is a DERIVATION from the market's own line one threshold down, not an upward empirical blend (France 6/6 games >=4 SOT is consistent but unused). The CAN-MOR -80.94 lesson does not bite: a market exists and sets the number.
